main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Wyckoff Transformer: a permutation-invariant autoregressive Transformer model without positional encoding, conditioned on space group symmetry, using Wyckoff positions as structure representation"],"problems":["Generation of highly symmetric crystal structures","Difficulty of generative models (especially diffusion models) in producing crystals with correct symmetry","Efficient exploration of the vast space of possible periodic atomic structures","Generation of stable and synthesizable materials"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Diversity of generated structures","Stability of generated structures","Formation energy prediction","Band gap prediction","Mechanical properties prediction","Thermal conductivity prediction"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First generative model for crystals conditioned on space group symmetry using Wyckoff positions as representation","Permutation-invariant autoregressive Transformer model without positional encoding for crystal generation","Best performance in generating novel, diverse, stable structures conditioned on symmetry space group","Competitive in predicting formation energy, band gap, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity compared to models not conditioned on symmetry"]}

methods: Wyckoff Transformer: a permutation-invariant autoregressive Transformer model without positional encoding, conditioned on space group symmetry, using Wyckoff positions as structure representation
problems: Generation of highly symmetric crystal structures
Difficulty of generative models (especially diffusion models) in producing crystals with correct symmetry
Efficient exploration of the vast space of possible periodic atomic structures
Generation of stable and synthesizable materials
novelty_claims: First generative model for crystals conditioned on space group symmetry using Wyckoff positions as representation
Permutation-invariant autoregressive Transformer model without positional encoding for crystal generation
Best performance in generating novel, diverse, stable structures conditioned on symmetry space group
Competitive in predicting formation energy, band gap, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity compared to models not conditioned on symmetry

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["CrystalFormer: a transformer-based autoregressive model for crystalline materials generation","Explicit modeling of space group symmetry via Wyckoff positions in the generative process","Autoregressive prediction of species and locations of symmetry-inequivalent atoms in the unit cell","Property-guided materials design using the generative model in a plug-and-play manner"],"problems":["Data- and compute-efficient generative modeling of crystalline materials","Incorporating space group symmetry into generative models for crystals","Efficient and generalizable initialization of symmetric crystal structures","Element substitution in crystal structures","Property-guided inverse design of crystalline materials"],"datasets":["Materials database of known inorganic compounds (implied, not explicitly named)"],"metrics":["Efficiency in symmetric structure initialization","Efficiency and generality in element substitution","Ability to match space group distribution of nature","Compression of the material dataset (statistical analysis)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["CrystalFormer is the first transformer-based autoregressive model that explicitly incorporates space group symmetry via Wyckoff positions for crystal generation.","The model treats Wyckoff positions as first-class citizens, rigorously integrating symmetry constraints into the generative process.","CrystalFormer enables data- and compute-efficient generative modeling by drastically reducing the search space of crystalline materials.","The approach allows for plug-and-play property-guided materials design, demonstrating flexibility and generality.","CrystalFormer demonstrates superior efficiency and generality in symmetric structure initialization and element substitution compared to conventional methods."]}

methods: CrystalFormer: a transformer-based autoregressive model for crystalline materials generation
Explicit modeling of space group symmetry via Wyckoff positions in the generative process
Autoregressive prediction of species and locations of symmetry-inequivalent atoms in the unit cell
Property-guided materials design using the generative model in a plug-and-play manner
problems: Data- and compute-efficient generative modeling of crystalline materials
Incorporating space group symmetry into generative models for crystals
Efficient and generalizable initialization of symmetric crystal structures
Element substitution in crystal structures
Property-guided inverse design of crystalline materials
datasets: Materials database of known inorganic compounds (implied, not explicitly named)
metrics: Efficiency and generality in element substitution
Ability to match space group distribution of nature
Compression of the material dataset (statistical analysis)
novelty_claims: CrystalFormer is the first transformer-based autoregressive model that explicitly incorporates space group symmetry via Wyckoff positions for crystal generation.
The model treats Wyckoff positions as first-class citizens, rigorously integrating symmetry constraints into the generative process.
CrystalFormer enables data- and compute-efficient generative modeling by drastically reducing the search space of crystalline materials.
The approach allows for plug-and-play property-guided materials design, demonstrating flexibility and generality.
CrystalFormer demonstrates superior efficiency and generality in symmetric structure initialization and element substitution compared to conventional methods.

methods: FlowMM: a pair of generative models for Crystal Structure Prediction (CSP) and De Novo Generation (DNG) based on Riemannian Flow Matching
Generalization of Riemannian Flow Matching to handle symmetries in crystals (translation, rotation, permutation, periodic boundary conditions)
Continuous Normalizing Flow (CNF) with finite time evolution for joint modeling of lattice parameters, atomic coordinates, and atom types
Symmetry-aware geodesic paths and natural base distributions for plausible lattice generation
Binary representation for atom types in DNG to reduce dimensionality
problems: Efficient and accurate generative modeling of periodic crystalline materials
Crystal Structure Prediction (CSP): predicting stable crystal structures for a given composition
De Novo Generation (DNG): generating both novel compositions and their stable crystal structures
Generating thermodynamically stable materials (low E_hull)
Overcoming inefficiencies and limitations of diffusion models for crystal generation
datasets: Two realistic crystal structure datasets (not named explicitly in the provided text)
metrics: Standard generative modeling metrics (not specified in the provided text)
Thermodynamic stability (E_hull, energy above convex hull)
Efficiency (number of integration steps required to generate stable materials)

results.value: FlowMM is about 3x more efficient than previous open methods
FlowMM generates materials with comparable stability to state-of-the-art diffusion models

novelty_claims: First generalization of Riemannian Flow Matching to estimate point cloud densities invariant to translation with periodic boundary conditions for crystals
Symmetry-aware generative modeling of crystals using continuous normalizing flows, enabling isometric invariances as inductive bias
Natural base distributions for lattice parameters, simplifying the generative process compared to diffusion models
Binary representation for atom types in DNG, drastically reducing dimensionality and improving accuracy in predicting unique elements per unit cell
Significantly faster inference (3x fewer integration steps) than diffusion models while maintaining or exceeding state-of-the-art performance

Decomposition of crystals into asymmetric units and symmetry transformations, learning their joint distribution via diffusion
Novel, interpretable representation of symmetry transformations as binary matrices for generalization across symmetry groups
problems: Generating novel crystalline materials with realistic and diverse symmetries

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Crystal-GFN: a generative model that sequentially samples crystal structural properties (space group, composition, lattice parameters) using a GFlowNet framework","Incorporation of physicochemical and structural hard constraints during sampling","Use of a machine learning proxy model to predict formation energy per atom as a reward function for training and sampling"],"problems":["Efficient generation of valid and diverse crystal structures with desirable physicochemical properties","Difficulty in searching the vast space of possible crystal structures for candidates with targeted properties (e.g., low formation energy)","Incorporating physical and structural constraints into generative models for crystals"],"datasets":["MatBench"],"metrics":["Predicted formation energy per atom","Diversity of sampled crystals","Percentage of samples below energy threshold"],"results":[{"metric":"Median predicted formation energy per atom","value":"-3.1 eV/atom"},{"metric":"Percentage of sampled structures with predicted energy < -2 eV/atom","value":"95%"}],"novelty_claims":["Crystal-GFN introduces a domain-inspired, sequential generative process for crystals, sampling space group, composition, and lattice parameters rather than atomic positions directly.","The approach flexibly incorporates physicochemical and structural hard constraints into the generative process.","Crystal-GFN leverages the GFlowNet framework to enable diverse sampling proportional to a reward function (e.g., predicted formation energy).","Demonstrates the ability to efficiently sample diverse, low-energy crystal structures using a machine learning proxy for formation energy."]}

methods: Crystal-GFN: a generative model that sequentially samples crystal structural properties (space group, composition, lattice parameters) using a GFlowNet framework
Incorporation of physicochemical and structural hard constraints during sampling
Use of a machine learning proxy model to predict formation energy per atom as a reward function for training and sampling
problems: Efficient generation of valid and diverse crystal structures with desirable physicochemical properties

results.metric: Median predicted formation energy per atom
Percentage of sampled structures with predicted energy < -2 eV/atom

novelty_claims: Crystal-GFN introduces a domain-inspired, sequential generative process for crystals, sampling space group, composition, and lattice parameters rather than atomic positions directly.
The approach flexibly incorporates physicochemical and structural hard constraints into the generative process.
Crystal-GFN leverages the GFlowNet framework to enable diverse sampling proportional to a reward function (e.g., predicted formation energy).
Demonstrates the ability to efficiently sample diverse, low-energy crystal structures using a machine learning proxy for formation energy.

novelty_claims: First demonstration that fine-tuned LLMs can generate stable inorganic materials as text with high physical validity.
Simple and effective method for encoding and generating crystal structures using LLMs.

Evidence that LLMs' pretrained biases are well-suited for atomistic data and that model scale improves symmetry learning.

Development of efficient algorithms to approximate infinite potential summations for crystal graphs
Integration of complete interatomic potentials into message passing neural networks for improved crystal property prediction

methods: Structure Representation Diffusion (StructRepDiff): a denoising diffusion model operating in a differentiable, physics-based, invariant representation space for periodic materials
Use of differentiable, physics-based structural descriptors as representations for periodic materials
Concatenation of structure descriptor with composition and cell vectors to form the final representation vector
Gradient-based reconstruction algorithm to recover 3D atomic structures from the representation space
problems: Generating high-quality periodic material structures that respect intrinsic symmetries, periodicity, and invariances
Overcoming data scarcity in materials datasets (limited and diverse data)
Avoiding issues with periodic boundaries and translational/rotational invariances in generative models
Simplifying the training process for generative models of periodic materials
novelty_claims: First example of periodic materials generation with diffusion models in the representation space rather than Cartesian space
Proposes a working invariant representation for materials generation that respects periodicity and invariance
Introduces a novel gradient-based reconstruction method for recovering 3D structures from the representation space
Simplifies the training process by avoiding the need for custom score functions or special considerations for invariances
Demonstrates competitive performance relative to existing state-of-the-art methods on established benchmarks

methods: Equivariant Graph Neural Network (EGNN) model that acts on both crystal lattice geometry and atomic positions

novelty_claims: First unified models (GemsNet and EGNN) that act simultaneously on both crystal lattice geometry and atomic positions for crystal material generation
GemsNet exploits all geometric information of atomic triplets within an equivariant GNN, enabling modification of both atomic positions and lattice geometry at the same time
Unified approach allows the model to reach optimal geometry during inference, which was not possible with previous models that only acted on either lattice or atomic positions separately
